title: 7 AI Trends Shaping Agentic Commerce in 2026
summary: Overview of the consumer and technology trends defining agentic commerce in 2026, including delegation of the purchase journey to AI agents within predefined boundaries.
relevance_to_sharp: Frames the shift from a human browsing a store to an agent executing the whole journey, the precondition for questioning physical/mental availability.

title: Agentic Commerce Is Redefining Retail - How to Respond
summary: BCG argues agentic commerce will reshape retail and estimates roughly $1.3 trillion of spending will be influenced, urging brands and retailers to respond now.
relevance_to_sharp: Quantifies how much purchasing moves through agents, the size of the market where the old availability rules are in question.

title: How agentic AI will reshape shopping in 2026
summary: eMarketer outlines how agentic AI changes discovery, comparison, and transaction behavior, with AI platforms projected to account for ~$20.9B in retail spending in 2026.
relevance_to_sharp: Documents the behavioral shift in how consumers discover and decide, the demand side of the availability question.

title: What Is Agentic Commerce? 45% of Shoppers Use AI (2026 Guide)
summary: Guide citing that a large share of shoppers now use AI in some part of the journey (product ideas 45%, review summaries 37%, price comparison 32%) and that ~70% are at least somewhat comfortable with an agent purchasing on their behalf.
key_quote: 73% of consumers are already using AI in their shopping journey
relevance_to_sharp: Adoption data showing humans increasingly delegating the very decisions Sharp's laws assumed they made themselves.

title: Agentic AI poised to disrupt retail, even with 50% of consumers cautious of fully autonomous purchases
summary: Bain finds agentic AI poised to redefine the customer journey while about half of shoppers remain cautious about fully autonomous purchases; 30-45% of US consumers already use GenAI for product research and comparison.
key_quote: around half of shoppers still say they are cautious about letting AI agents autonomously handle purchases from start to finish
relevance_to_sharp: Shows delegation is partial and trust-gated, defining which decisions still involve a human and which do not.

title: Agentic shopping, outcome-driven AI, and creator commerce: Takeaways from Shoptalk 2026
summary: Industry takeaways from Shoptalk 2026 on how agentic shopping and outcome-driven AI are changing the retail conversation.
relevance_to_sharp: Industry-consensus signal on how fast agent-mediated shopping is moving from concept to practice.

title: The rise of agentic shopping: Why challenger brands will win the AI era
summary: Argues AI agents do not reward incumbency the way human memory does; they weigh attribute-level data, so challenger brands with better structured data can win consideration.
key_quote: AI agents are immune to nostalgia
relevance_to_sharp: Directly challenges mental availability: if agents are immune to brand nostalgia and salience, the mental side of Sharp's model may not transfer.

title: The Era of Agentic Commerce: A Strategic Framework for the Dual-Interface Brand
summary: Proposes brands must serve a dual interface, one for humans and one for AI agents, shifting from SEO to agentic optimization and deploying brand agents that operate continuously in commerce ecosystems.
key_quote: AI agents prioritize the specific parameters of a user's prompt, requiring granular, attribute-level data rather than broad, mass-appeal marketing claims
relevance_to_sharp: Reframes distinctive assets and salience as machine-readable attributes, a candidate replacement for mental availability.

title: Agentic commerce: How AI shopping agents can change retail (the automation curve)
summary: McKinsey maps an automation curve for how much of the purchase decision consumers hand to agents by category, and what it means for brand consideration.
relevance_to_sharp: Provides a category-by-category view of where agent decisioning displaces human consideration, the gradient along which Sharp's laws weaken.

title: Agentic Commerce: Make Your Brand Unmissable
summary: Accenture argues brands must become legible and trustworthy to agents, and reports rising fraud and trust concerns: 78% of financial institutions expect a rise in AI-agent-linked fraud.
key_quote: 87% of CTOs and heads of payments at financial institutions believe that trust will be the most significant barrier to the adoption of agentic payments
relevance_to_sharp: Replaces 'be salient' with 'be machine-legible and trusted', a reformulation of availability for agents.

title: Preparing Your Brand for Agentic AI
summary: HBR guidance on how brands should adapt positioning, data, and measurement when AI agents mediate discovery and purchase.
relevance_to_sharp: A management-press articulation of how brand-building changes when the buyer is an agent.

title: Agentic commerce: Compete in an AI-buyer world
summary: PwC frames the 'AI-buyer world' and what brands and retailers must do to remain competitive when an agent is the buyer.
relevance_to_sharp: Names the core premise of the keynote, an AI buyer, and its competitive implications.

title: The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants
summary: McKinsey sizes the agentic commerce opportunity (as much as $1T orchestrated US retail revenue and $3-5T globally by 2030) and describes the new consumer-merchant dynamic.
key_quote: agentic commerce could generate as much as $1 trillion in orchestrated US retail revenue by 2030
relevance_to_sharp: Headline market-size case for why the rules-of-the-game question matters commercially.

title: When AI meets desire: Innovating human-centered luxury experiences in the agentic age
summary: Explores how luxury and desire-driven categories, which depend on emotion and brand aura, navigate an agentic age where agents optimize on attributes.
relevance_to_sharp: Tests the limits of the thesis: categories built on emotion and mental availability may resist agent flattening most.

title: 2030 Forecast: How Agentic AI Will Reshape US Retail
summary: Bain forecasts the US agentic commerce market at roughly $300-500B by 2030, equal to 15-25% of eCommerce, and distinguishes third-party, on-site, and off-site agents.
key_quote: third-party 'objective' agents pose the greatest threat to disintermediate retailers
relevance_to_sharp: Identifies who controls the agent surface, the new 'shelf', and who risks being disintermediated.

title: Agentic commerce (merchant guide)
summary: Shopify's merchant-facing explainer on agentic commerce and how its merchants can sell through AI agents and stay discoverable.
relevance_to_sharp: Platform view of how millions of merchants get represented to agents, the supply side of agent availability.

title: Agentic Payments in B2C Commerce: Where We Are Now
summary: Forrester's status check on agentic payments in B2C, assessing readiness across the discovery-to-checkout flow.
relevance_to_sharp: Reality check on how far agent-led transactions actually reach today versus the hype.

title: Bain & Company Forecasts Up to $500 Billion by 2030 for Agentic Commerce
summary: Secondary analysis of Bain's forecast, emphasizing that retailers must now serve both human and AI-agent customers.
key_quote: Retailers Must Now Serve Both Human and AI Agent Customers
relevance_to_sharp: Crystallizes the dual-customer reality: brands now market to humans and to agents simultaneously.

title: Buy it in ChatGPT: Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol
summary: OpenAI launches Instant Checkout in ChatGPT and, with Stripe, releases the Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP) as an open standard for agent-initiated purchases.
key_quote: If a merchant already processes payments with Stripe, they can enable agentic payments in as little as one line of code
relevance_to_sharp: The new point of purchase: the buy now happens inside the agent's conversation, not on a shelf or a brand's site.

title: Supporting additional payment methods for agentic commerce
summary: Stripe describes its Agentic Commerce Suite covering discovery, checkout, payments, and fraud handling through a single integration so businesses can sell on AI agents.
relevance_to_sharp: Infrastructure that makes a brand transactable by an agent, a literal form of physical availability for machines.

title: Visa Intelligent Commerce: Enabling AI agents to buy securely and seamlessly
summary: Visa Intelligent Commerce embeds payment credentials, controls, authentication, and protections into agent-initiated transactions; Visa Intelligent Commerce Connect offers one integration supporting four agent protocols, with Akamai providing bot and behavioral intelligence under the Trusted Agent Protocol.
key_quote: Visa's product supports payments initiated through four major agent protocols: Trusted Agent Protocol, Machine Payments Protocol, Agentic Commerce Protocol and Universal Commerce Protocol
relevance_to_sharp: Establishes agent identity and trust rails, deciding which agents are allowed to transact and how brands are reached.

title: Visa and Mastercard both launch new agentic AI payments tools
summary: Coverage of Visa and Mastercard simultaneously launching agentic AI payment tools, including Mastercard Agent Pay with tokenized credentials and Payment Passkeys.
relevance_to_sharp: The card networks racing to own agent trust rails signals how central infrastructure is to agent-era availability.

title: Adyen Announces Adyen Agentic as the Universal Translator for the Next Era of Commerce
summary: Adyen launches Adyen Agentic, modular APIs (Agentic Feed, Agentic Cart, Agentic Payments) that let enterprises sell through conversational AI platforms without rebuilding commerce systems per channel; early partners include Amex, Mastercard, Salesforce, Visa, and retailers ESW, Scheels, Sezane, and SharkNinja.
key_quote: the universal translator for the next era of commerce
relevance_to_sharp: The 'Agentic Feed' layer is, in effect, how a brand's distinctive assets get translated into the structured data an agent reads.

title: Visa opens one integration for AI agent payments
summary: Reports on Visa Intelligent Commerce Connect giving merchants, agent builders, and payment enablers a single integration into agentic commerce across major protocols.
relevance_to_sharp: Consolidation toward one integration lowers the barrier for brands to become agent-transactable.

title: Agentic Commerce: When AI Buys on Your Behalf, Who Pays, Who's Liable?
summary: Examines the unresolved liability question when an agent makes an unauthorized or harmful purchase, across consumer, AI provider, merchant, and platform.
key_quote: when an AI agent makes an unauthorised or harmful purchase, who is liable, the consumer, the AI provider, the merchant or the platform?
relevance_to_sharp: Trust and liability become preconditions for delegation, gating how far the agent-buyer model can spread.

title: Agentic commerce: The next legal frontier in AI-powered shopping
summary: Legal analysis of contract formation, consent, and consumer-rights questions raised when agents transact on a consumer's behalf.
relevance_to_sharp: Clarifies whether an agent's purchase forms a binding contract, foundational to the agent-buyer premise.

title: Agentic AI Commerce: The Next Wave of Online Shopping and Retailer Risk
summary: Surveys retailer risk in agentic commerce, including manipulation, mispurchase, and the allocation of liability among consumers, merchants, and AI providers.
key_quote: protecting against AI agent manipulation (25% vulnerability rate)
relevance_to_sharp: Manipulation risk reframes 'persuasion', the soft core of mental availability, as an attack surface rather than a marketing lever.

title: Agentic Commerce Is Coming, but Regulation Meant for Humans Will Slow It Down
summary: Argues existing human-oriented rules (consent, disclosure, authentication) create friction for agent transactions and that no jurisdiction yet regulates agentic commerce specifically.
key_quote: no jurisdiction has enacted regulation specifically addressing agentic commerce
relevance_to_sharp: Shows the rulebook itself is being rewritten, the literal 'rules of the game' your keynote addresses.

title: EU AI Act: What It Means for Agentic Commerce
summary: Reviews how the EU AI Act, PSD3, GDPR, and the Consumer Rights Directive overlap on agentic commerce without clearly resolving authorization and liability.
relevance_to_sharp: European regulatory lens relevant to the keynote audience and to how agent availability will be governed.

title: Microsoft propels retail forward with agentic AI capabilities for every retail function
summary: Microsoft unveils agentic AI for retail including Copilot Checkout (buy inside Copilot) and Brand Agents for Shopify merchants, enabling conversational discovery, recommendations and outfit building.
relevance_to_sharp: Brand Agents and Copilot Checkout turn the brand into a conversational agent surface, replacing shelf presence with in-assistant presence.

title: Microsoft introduces agentic AI tools to automate retail and B2B commerce operations
summary: Copilot Checkout lets shoppers buy inside Copilot, integrating PayPal, Shopify and Stripe, with launch brands including Urban Outfitters, Anthropologie and Etsy sellers.
key_quote: Copilot Checkout ... enables shoppers to complete purchases directly within the Copilot app, eliminating the need to redirect to external websites
relevance_to_sharp: The transaction moves inside the assistant; being easy to buy now means being integrated into the agents checkout, not on a retailers site.

title: Gartner Predicts 60% of Brands Will Use Agentic AI to Deliver Streamlined One-to-One Interactions by 2028
summary: Gartner forecasts that by 2028, 60% of brands will use agentic AI to facilitate streamlined one-to-one customer interactions.
key_quote: By 2028, 60% of brands will use agentic AI to facilitate streamlined one-to-one interactions
relevance_to_sharp: If brands shift to one-to-one agent interactions, mass mental-availability reach gives way to personalised, agent-mediated engagement.

title: Gartner: AI agents will command $15 trillion in B2B purchases by 2028
summary: Gartner forecasts AI agents will intermediate more than $15 trillion in B2B spending by 2028, with autonomous buying, machine-to-machine negotiation and data-verification frameworks going mainstream.
key_quote: AI agents will intermediate more than $15 trillion in B2B spending by 2028
relevance_to_sharp: Extends the agent-buyer question to B2B, where machine-to-machine negotiation replaces relationship and brand salience entirely.

title: Agentic Commerce Impact Could Reach $385 Billion by 2030
summary: Morgan Stanley estimates agentic shoppers could represent $190-385 billion of US e-commerce by 2030 (10-20% share), with groceries and CPG the largest growth driver; about 23% of Americans bought using AI in the past month.
key_quote: Agentic shoppers could represent $190 billion to $385 billion in U.S. e-commerce spending by 2030
relevance_to_sharp: Confirms agent buying concentrates first in low-emotion replenishment categories, where mental availability mattered least.

title: ChatGPT Commerce & Agentic Shopping Statistics 2026
summary: Aggregated data showing ChatGPT drives about 20% of Walmarts and Etsys referral traffic and about 15% of Targets (Similarweb), with most agentic activity on product and search pages rather than checkout.
key_quote: ChatGPT now drives 20% of Walmart's referral traffic
relevance_to_sharp: Shows the agent becoming the discovery surface (the new shelf), redirecting where physical availability must be won.

title: Akamai AI Brand Presence
summary: Akamai product for controlling how AI systems read, interpret and present a brands content: detects AI agents at the edge, serves structured machine-readable content, and tracks brand visibility and citations across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and Perplexity (AI Engine Optimization).
key_quote: AI is becoming the primary entry point to your brand, shaping how users engage before they ever visit your website.
relevance_to_sharp: Reframes mental availability for the machine age: brand presence becomes structured, machine-readable content agents parse, not memory structures in a human mind.

title: Akamai AI Brand Presence - Product Brief
summary: Product brief detailing Akamai AI Brand Presence: real-time edge detection of AI agents, delivery of optimized structured content without CMS changes, and a dashboard for AI traffic, referrals and citations.
key_quote: AI systems don't browse like humans. They parse, summarize, and generate responses, which requires structured, machine-readable content.
relevance_to_sharp: Direct evidence for the thesis: winning the agent requires machine-readable structured content, the agent-era successor to distinctive brand assets.

title: The Fold - Agentic Commerce Platform
summary: Vendor site for The Fold, a live reasoning substrate for AI agents to make and maintain commerce decisions as prices, inventory and policies change; positions structured, maintainable decision surfaces as core agentic-commerce infrastructure and claims 50%+ of digital commerce will be agent-mediated by 2027.
key_quote: For 25 years we built catalogs for humans scrolling through filters. Now AI agents translate intent, weigh constraints, and make commitments on behalf of users.
relevance_to_sharp: Crystallises the shift from human-facing catalogs (physical and mental availability) to machine-facing decision surfaces that agents reason over.

title: The Complete Product Data Optimization Guide for Google's AI Shopping (2026)
summary: Argues product data completeness is now decisive for AI-shopping visibility: stores with 99.9% attribute completion (a 'Golden Record') see 3-4x higher visibility in AI recommendations than stores with sparse data; details new Google Merchant Center attributes built for conversational commerce.
key_quote: Stores with 99.9% attribute completion (what the industry calls a "Golden Record") are seeing 3-4x higher visibility in AI recommendations compared to stores with sparse data.
relevance_to_sharp: Quantifies the new physical/mental availability hybrid: feed completeness is now a measurable, rankable substitute for shelf placement and salience.

title: Walmart's Open AI Strategy vs Amazon: 2026 Agentic Commerce Playbook
summary: Contrasts Walmart's open-partnership strategy (Sparky agent plus integrations with ChatGPT, Gemini and the Universal Commerce Protocol) with Amazon's closed-ecosystem approach centred on proprietary 'Buy for Me' and limited external AI integration.
key_quote: Walmart plugs into external AI ecosystems (ChatGPT, Gemini, Google's Universal Commerce Protocol) while also building internal agents.
relevance_to_sharp: A live test of two competing bets on where the new 'shelf' lives: be everywhere agents are (Walmart) versus own the agent entirely (Amazon).

title: Perplexity "Buy with Pro": zero-fee shopping vs OpenAI's 4% Agentic Commerce Protocol
summary: Perplexity charges merchants zero fees for in-chat checkout via PayPal, monetising through Pro subscriptions instead; contrasts with OpenAI's 4% ACP transaction fee, which stalled merchant adoption and led OpenAI to scale back Instant Checkout in March 2026.
key_quote: Perplexity charges merchants nothing.
relevance_to_sharp: Shows the agent layer itself is not neutral infrastructure but a contested business model; whichever platform wins distribution sets new terms for being 'available'.

title: Research: Traditional Marketing Doesn't Work on AI Shopping Agents
summary: Across thousands of simulated shopping rounds over four leading AI models and four product categories, classic human-persuasion tactics (scarcity, countdown timers, bundling, strike-through pricing) failed to reliably influence AI shopping agents and sometimes backfired; only authentic star ratings consistently increased choice, while price reliably decreased it.
key_quote: In thousands of simulated shopping rounds... only star ratings consistently increased choice in the expected direction, while price reliably decreased it.
relevance_to_sharp: Direct experimental evidence for the thesis: the persuasion machinery mental availability relies on does not transfer to agents; only verifiable signals (ratings, price) move them.

title: LinkedIn post: Two HBR pieces worth reading together
summary: Byron Sharp himself comments on the agentic-commerce shift, framing AI agents as a new distribution intermediary comparable to historical ones (bar staff, hardware sales people, doctors, mortgage brokers) and characterising panic about AI as generational hyperbole.
relevance_to_sharp: The namesake of the framework this paper engages with, commenting directly and in real time on whether his own laws survive the agentic shift.

title: LinkedIn post on HBR AI shopping agent research
summary: Commentary on the HBR persuasion-tactics research, distilling the finding into a single strategic line about brand strength versus algorithmic persuasion.
key_quote: The question isn't whether your brand can persuade an algorithm. It's whether it's strong enough not to need to.
relevance_to_sharp: Crystallises the bifurcation argument in one sentence: brand strength (mental availability) still matters, but as a foundation, not as a persuasion lever aimed at the agent.

title: Your Next Customer Is a Machine
summary: Argues agents are replacing the click economy: opens with a Shanghai agent (Meituan's Xiaomei) autonomously ordering and timing a woman's lunch; reports Walmart's ~200,000-product ChatGPT experiment converted at one third the rate of Walmart's own site (due to wrong delivery estimates and shipping costs) and that ByteDance's agent phone sold 30,000 units before WeChat, Taobao and Alipay moved to restrict it; frames the endpoint as a 'Coasean Singularity' where only trust and risk management remain as core business functions.
key_quote: Machine-readable trust. In the old world, a brand told a story to a human. Now it starts filing a specification to an agent.
relevance_to_sharp: A vivid, concrete case (with a real failure) showing physical availability relocating to data accuracy (delivery estimates, shipping costs) and mental availability collapsing into verifiable transaction history.

title: LinkedIn post: AI leadership and the CPG existential threat
summary: Argues CPG brands face an existential threat as AI agents mediate purchasing; cites Walmart, Unilever, Mars, PepsiCo and Mondelez already adapting, and Google's three new Merchant Center tools (Conversational Attributes, AI Performance Insights, Universal Cart) as formalising the competitive shift from shelf to agent conversation.
key_quote: I barely walk into a supermarket anymore. My agent does it.
relevance_to_sharp: A first-person account of physical availability becoming irrelevant to the human entirely, delegated wholesale to the agent doing the shelf-walking.

title: How we're helping retailers thrive with new Universal Commerce Protocol features and AI tools on Google
summary: Google launches three Merchant Center tools for agentic shopping: Conversational Attributes (natural-language product descriptions for AI Mode/Gemini matching), AI Performance Insights (share-of-voice on AI surfaces vs competitors), and Universal Cart (cross-retailer checkout via Search/Gemini with Google Pay); early partners include Nike, Sephora, Target, Ulta Beauty, Walmart, Wayfair and Shopify merchants.
key_quote: Our new Universal Cart is a truly intelligent shopping cart that works across retailers and across services like Search, Gemini and more.
relevance_to_sharp: Google formalising 'share of voice on AI surfaces' as a trackable metric is the platform-level institutionalisation of machine-readable mental availability.

title: Mondelez Overhauls $3.5B Digital Commerce Strategy for the AI Search Era
summary: Mondelez projects 20-30% of its product purchases will happen via AI agents within one to two years and has hired a global lead of emerging commerce platforms dedicated to agentic commerce, running pilots and building a scalable playbook for agent-mediated discovery, recommendation and transaction.
key_quote: 20-30% of Mondelez product purchases will happen via AI agents within 1-2 years
relevance_to_sharp: A named, large FMCG brand putting an internal number and a dedicated role behind the shift, evidence the FMCG implications in this paper are already operational, not speculative.

title: LinkedIn post: Meta and Zuck are officially in trouble
summary: Argues Meta has squandered its AR/smart-glasses lead to Google, which is integrating Gemini AI into upcoming smart glasses; includes a demo of Gemini autonomously ordering coffee and managing a calendar via voice. Tangential to commerce strategy directly, but a concrete consumer-facing demo of agent-initiated ordering.
relevance_to_sharp: A live demonstration of the wearable/voice surface where agent-initiated purchasing (ordering coffee) may eventually happen outside any screen at all.

title: Research: What China's AI Agents Reveal About the Future of Commerce
summary: Argues that agentic commerce in China has moved past assistive chatbots into full transaction orchestration. Meituan's Xiaomei agent, launched late 2025, is described by the platform itself as 'an orchestrator plus execution agent': a user can say something like 'order my usual lunch, but deliver it 20 minutes later today,' and the system interprets intent, applies stored preferences and completes the purchase, often with no screen interaction at all.
key_quote: Xiaomei is built as an orchestrator plus execution agent, not a convenience layer: it interprets intent, applies stored preferences and completes the transaction, often with no screen interaction at all.
relevance_to_sharp: When an agent already holds a shopper's stored preferences and executes the order itself, there is no screen moment left for salience or distinctiveness to act on; the brand has to have already won a place inside those stored preferences, well before the transaction starts.

title: Buy it in ChatGPT: Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol
summary: OpenAI's own demo video for Instant Checkout, the feature (built on the open Agentic Commerce Protocol) that let US ChatGPT users buy directly from Etsy sellers and Shopify merchants such as Glossier, SKIMS, Spanx and Vuori without leaving the chat. Note: by March 2026 OpenAI had narrowed this, shifting ChatGPT's shopping focus back toward product discovery rather than in-chat checkout, so treat this video as the launch moment rather than the current state of the product.
relevance_to_sharp: A concrete, on-screen example of physical availability being rebuilt as a protocol: the agent completes the purchase inside the conversation, so the merchant's catalogue has to already be machine-readable before the moment a shopper asks.

title: From Recommendations to Delegation: A Systematic Review Mapping Agentic AI in E-Commerce and Its Consumer Effects
summary: Systematic review of the academic literature mapping the progression of agentic AI in e-commerce—from assistive product recommendations through to fully delegated purchasing—and synthesising findings on how consumer trust, autonomy preferences, and decision-making change at each stage.
relevance_to_sharp: Provides the academic evidence base for the delegation gradient along which Sharp's availability laws erode: the review maps exactly which consumer decisions have already been delegated and which remain human-gated.

title: AI traffic grows but retail sites lag in AI search visibility
summary: Adobe Digital Insights analysis of 1 trillion+ US retail site visits finds AI-sourced traffic grew 393% year-over-year in Q1 2026 and now converts 42% better than non-AI traffic—a complete reversal from Q1 2025 when AI traffic converted 38% worse; yet most retail sites remain insufficiently machine-readable to capitalise on the surge.
relevance_to_sharp: Quantifies the speed at which AI surfaces are becoming the primary discovery channel, and identifies machine-readability—not brand salience—as the decisive variable separating visible from invisible brands in the agent era.

title: The future of commerce: Agentic shopping in Asia Pacific
summary: Deloitte survey across Asia Pacific finds 29% of consumer businesses are already adopting agentic AI, expected to surge to 76% within two years; nine in ten retail executives expect AI to supersede traditional search by 2026, while two-thirds of leaders don't expect customers to embrace fully autonomous purchasing before 2028; machine-readable product catalogues are identified as the pivotal differentiator.
key_quote: The biggest hurdle for legacy retailers is ensuring a robust data catalog that is machine-readable, including details on product attributes, inventory availability, pricing, promos and even policies—this will make the difference in being seen or being looked over in agentic commerce.
relevance_to_sharp: APAC data—from a region driving two-thirds of global new retail sales—shows a faster delegation curve than Western markets, with machine-readable availability already the competitive dividing line.

title: OpenAI's first try at agentic shopping stumbled. It's trying again
summary: CNBC reports that OpenAI's Instant Checkout—which allowed purchases directly inside ChatGPT—was scaled back in early 2026 due to poor merchant conversion and limited consumer uptake; OpenAI is rebuilding its commerce strategy around product discovery with click-through to retailer sites, and dedicated retailer apps within ChatGPT.
relevance_to_sharp: The stumble illustrates the consumer-trust gap between AI-assisted discovery (high adoption) and fully delegated purchasing (low adoption)—the trust gradient that governs how fast Sharp's laws actually erode in practice.

title: Europe's agentic commerce moment: Decision influence is here; execution is coming
summary: Survey of 749 consumers across France, Germany, and the UK finds more than 50% of consumer journeys are already influenced by LLMs, AI search, or digital agents, but fully autonomous execution remains rare; McKinsey frames Europe as in a 'decision influence' phase—agent-driven transactions are coming but not yet dominant.
key_quote: More than 50 percent of consumer journeys are already influenced by large language models (LLMs), AI search, or digital agents.
relevance_to_sharp: Maps exactly where European consumers sit on the delegation curve—agents influence decisions but do not yet execute them—establishing the timeline for when Sharp's mental-availability model fully gives way to agent-availability.

title: Agentic Commerce's Success Centers On Overcoming Key Frontiers In Consumer Trust
summary: Forrester identifies consumer trust as the binding constraint on agentic commerce scaling, cataloguing unresolved frontiers in agent authentication, delegation consent, transparency about AI decision criteria, and dispute resolution—all of which brands and platforms must address before mass autonomous purchasing occurs.
relevance_to_sharp: Trust is the gate through which mental availability must pass to become agent-delegatable behaviour; brands must build credibility with both consumers and the agents representing them, not just with human attention.

title: Agentic AI is reshaping retail and consumer goods economics
summary: Microsoft analyses how agentic AI restructures retail economics—compressing margins on commodity products where agents optimise purely on price while creating premium opportunities for brands with strong, differentiated data quality and authentic positioning that agents can recognise and recommend.
relevance_to_sharp: Provides the economic framing for why the thesis matters commercially: margin compression is the concrete financial consequence for brands that cannot translate their distinctive assets into machine-readable form.

title: Amazon offers its AI shopping tech to outside retailers in new phase of agentic commerce race
summary: Amazon opens the AWS Agentic Shopping Assistant—built on the same technology as Rufus, which drove nearly $12 billion in incremental Amazon sales—to third-party retailers, enabling them to deploy AI shopping assistants on their own e-commerce sites within 60 days; Tapestry's Kate Spade AI Gift Concierge is an early deployment.
key_quote: built on the same technology that powers the Alexa for Shopping assistant on Amazon.com, formerly known as Rufus, which the company says drove nearly $12 billion in incremental sales last year
relevance_to_sharp: Amazon externalising its agent commerce infrastructure signals that AI-mediated shopping is becoming commodity plumbing—the 'new shelf' is now a managed service any retailer can subscribe to, not a proprietary advantage.

title: Agentic AI Commerce: The Next Retail Revolution Is Here
summary: Bain, in partnership with Stripe, argues that agentic commerce constitutes a genuine retail revolution and that full agent-to-agent (A2A) commerce—where buyer and seller AI agents transact directly—could eventually bypass classic retail websites entirely; the winning strategy for retailers is a blend of owned agents and openness to third-party agents, not a binary choice.
key_quote: Full agent-to-agent (A2A) commerce, in which the buyer's and retailer's AI agents transact directly, could eventually shortcut or bypass classic retail websites altogether.
relevance_to_sharp: Introduces A2A commerce as the logical endpoint—where neither human presence nor brand salience enters the transaction at all—framing the outer limit of how far Sharp's model must be reconstructed.

title: The State Of Agentic Commerce In Mid-2026
summary: Forrester's mid-year reality check finds most agentic experiences remain assistive rather than truly autonomous; third-party answer engines are stealing traffic from independent marketplaces unable to match AI-aggregated assortments from Etsy or Shopify; Forrester predicts five major US or European brands will unify agentic commerce experiences by year-end.
key_quote: Most of today's 'agentic' experiences are, in fact, merely assistive, not truly autonomous.
relevance_to_sharp: Calibrates the actual pace of autonomy adoption—hype outpaces behaviour—giving a realistic timeline for when agent-mediated purchasing reaches the scale at which Sharp's laws are fully superseded.

title: Millions of merchants can sell in AI chats
summary: Shopify reports that AI-driven traffic to its merchants grew 8× year-over-year in Q1 2026 while orders from AI-powered search increased nearly 13×; millions of Shopify merchants now have a managed presence across ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google AI Mode, and the Gemini app through Agentic Storefronts.
relevance_to_sharp: The 8× traffic and 13× order figures quantify the rate at which AI surfaces are replacing the traditional 'shelf'—and Shopify making this multi-agent presence the default for its merchants shows how quickly agent availability is becoming table stakes.

title: Mastercard Launches Agent Suite to Ready Enterprises for a New Era
summary: Mastercard launches Agent Suite—a package combining technical integration support, customisable AI agents, data-fuelled insights, and immersive 'Spark' strategic workshops—to help enterprises adopt agentic AI for product discovery and conversational shopping; available Q2 2026 through Mastercard's global advisory network of 4,000 advisors.
relevance_to_sharp: A payment network building enterprise infrastructure for agentic commerce signals that the authorisation rails for agent-mediated transactions are actively being laid—making 'agent availability' a near-term commercial requirement, not a future concept.

title: Mastercard Launches Agent Pay for Machines to Unlock Super-Fast, Always-On Payments
summary: Agent Pay for Machines (AP4M) enables high-frequency, micro-value AI-to-AI transactions with Verifiable Intent credentialing, programmatic spending limits (human-set permissions stored on blockchain—Polygon, Solana, Base), and guaranteed multi-rail settlement across cards, accounts, and stablecoins; 30+ launch partners include Adyen, Ant International, Cloudflare, Coinbase, and Stripe.
key_quote: Mastercard's protocol stores permissions that humans grant their AI agents onto a blockchain
relevance_to_sharp: AP4M represents the full disintermediation endpoint: AI-to-AI transactions at machine speed require brand trust to be encoded at the protocol and credentialing layer—there is no human salience moment remaining in the purchase flow.

title: Powering Product Discovery in ChatGPT
summary: OpenAI expands the Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP) for product discovery inside ChatGPT—adding visual browsing, side-by-side comparisons, and live inventory data—with Target, Sephora, Nordstrom, Lowe's, Best Buy, Home Depot, and Wayfair as early retail partners; Shopify merchants are automatically integrated via Shopify Catalog.
relevance_to_sharp: OpenAI pivoting from in-chat checkout to conversational product discovery defines the current form of physical availability for agents: being findable, comparable, and accurately represented in the conversation interface is the new prerequisite for consideration.

title: Adobe Commerce commits to agentic commerce standards
summary: Adobe Commerce announces support for both Google's Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) and OpenAI's Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP), making merchant catalogues, pricing, and inventory machine-readable for Gemini and ChatGPT agents; capabilities roll out progressively through 2026 via existing order-management integrations.
relevance_to_sharp: A major enterprise commerce platform endorsing both open protocols means millions of brands will gain agent-readable product data by default—machine-readable availability becomes infrastructure, not a differentiator.

title: New tech and tools for retailers to succeed in an agentic shopping era
summary: Google announces the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) as an open standard for agentic commerce, co-developed with Shopify, Etsy, Wayfair, Target, and Walmart, and endorsed by 20+ ecosystem partners including Adyen, American Express, Best Buy, Mastercard, Stripe, and Visa; also introduces the Business Agent enabling conversational brand-to-shopper interactions directly in Search.
relevance_to_sharp: UCP as an open, multi-stakeholder protocol defines the new physical-availability infrastructure—being protocol-compliant and exposing a machine-readable catalogue replaces being on the shelf as the prerequisite for purchase.

title: Agentic AI and consumers
summary: The CMA publishes practical guidance and a policy paper establishing that existing consumer protection law (DMCCA 2024, Consumer Rights Act 2015) applies fully to AI agents—businesses remain liable for agent behaviour even when AI is third-party supplied; agents must not mislead on pricing, refunds, or cancellation rights; breaches can draw fines up to 10% of global annual turnover.
key_quote: businesses remain fully responsible for what their AI agents say and do, even where the technology is supplied or designed by a third party
relevance_to_sharp: The first authoritative regulatory guidance from a major competition authority establishes the trust and liability framework that will govern agent-mediated commerce—brands cannot outsource accountability to AI vendors.

title: AI agents under antitrust and consumer protection scrutiny: Risks and opportunities for compliance
summary: Hogan Lovells maps dual antitrust and consumer-protection exposure in agentic commerce: AI agents optimising on price can inadvertently signal price-setting to competitors (the CMA's 'agentic collusion' risk); the DOJ and FTC have active positions on algorithmic price-setting; compliance teams must coordinate antitrust and consumer functions rather than treating them separately.
relevance_to_sharp: Antitrust exposure reframes how brands can compete in an agent-mediated world: agents that transparently optimise on price can simultaneously create market-homogenisation risks that attract regulator scrutiny, constraining the competitive angles available.

title: Agentic AI: The liability gap your contracts may not cover
summary: Clifford Chance identifies a systemic liability gap in enterprise contracts for agentic commerce: legacy technology agreements were written for deterministic systems and do not allocate responsibility for autonomous AI actions; disputes on agent-initiated card transactions are running at approximately 2.4× the rate of comparable human-initiated card-not-present transactions.
key_quote: disputes on agent-initiated transactions are running at roughly 2.4x the rate of comparable human-initiated card-not-present transactions
relevance_to_sharp: The 2.4× dispute rate is concrete evidence that trust infrastructure—not brand salience—is the gating variable for agentic commerce scaling; until liability is resolved, brands face a structural deterrent to full delegation.

title: How Agentic AI Will Reshape Payments
summary: IMF Note proposes a three-layer architecture for agentic payments—AI intent/orchestration, deterministic authorisation/control, and guaranteed settlement—arguing that AI agents must be confined to the intent layer while authorisation and settlement remain rule-governed to prevent uncontrolled autonomous spending and systemic risk.
key_quote: The main risk with agentic payments does not come from using probability-based reasoning itself, but from letting adaptive systems make irreversible payments without proper controls, checks, or accountability.
relevance_to_sharp: The IMF entering the conversation with a macroprudential framework signals that agentic commerce has crossed into financial-stability territory—the architecture of who controls the payment layer will determine which brands remain reachable by autonomous agents.
